positional_bias.notes: Perfect uniform spread across all four positions. Sidecar correct_position_distribution matches quiz.gift exactly — no meta_gift_desync.

length_bias.notes: No question has a correct answer whose length exceeds 120% of the mean distractor length. Zero longest-correct cases out of 4 MCQs.

per_distractor_scores.text_prefix: A new vector with the same number of positions...
A matrix made from all the possible pairs...
A list of products, one for each matching pair...
0.56, because you only multiply the first positions...
0.46, because you add the vectors first...
1.10, because you sum all six numbers...
A large positive score, because right angles create the most overlap...
A large negative score, because right angles indicate opposing vectors...
A score of exactly 1, because perpendicular vectors are fully independent...
...the song vector is longer, so it contains more musical information...
...recently added to the library and ranked by upload order...
...shares exactly the same number of dimensions as the listener's taste vector...

distractor_plausibility.notes: No absurd (score=1) distractors. Two distractors scored 2 (wrong but not a canonical beginner misconception): q3's 'exactly 1' option and q4's 'upload order' option. These are weak but not absurd — they do not constitute free points. absurd_rate=0.00 well under the 0.25 threshold.

coverage_map.lo: LO-1: Explain dot product in plain language (multiply matching positions, sum)
LO-2: Calculate dot product step by step
LO-4: Interpret high dot product as similarity signal

objective_coverage.notes: All 5 LOs covered. q4 objective_ref explicitly references both LO-4 and LO-5; fuzzy match confirmed for all mappings. No orphan questions.

findings.location: q4, distractor: '...recently added to the library and ranked by upload order...'
findings.note: This distractor is not a canonical beginner misconception arising from the topic — 'upload order' has no conceptual link to dot product instruction. It is plain-language and not harmful, but a stronger distractor grounded in a taught concept (e.g., magnitude sensitivity from §5.6) would be preferable. Rolled into §3 plausibility signal (score 2).

beginner_scope.notes: No undefined jargon in any stem. All questions test only concepts taught in this topic. No hard out_of_scope_question findings. One soft weak_distractor finding in q4 does not trigger a hard fail.

verdict: PASS — all five checks clean. Positional distribution is perfectly uniform (max_share=0.25). No length bias (longest_correct_rate=0.00). No absurd distractors (absurd_rate=0.00). All five Learning Objectives covered with no orphans. All stems are beginner-accessible and on-scope. One soft finding: q4 has a weak distractor ('upload order') that is not a canonical misconception but is not absurd and does not trigger failure.
